concerns.text: FR-003 mandates Mann-Whitney U tests between 'Control and MCI/AD groups'. This contradicts US-2 Acceptance Scenario 2 which specifies 'Control and AD groups' and the Edge Cases which imply a binary comparison. Merging MCI and AD into a single group without explicit statistical justification in the spec creates a contradiction in the defined hypothesis test structure.

US-3 and FR-004 mandate a 'stratified 70/15/15 train/validation/test split'. However, SC-003 and US-3 Acceptance Scenario 3 mandate '5-fold stratified cross-validation'. These are mutually exclusive validation strategies for the same model training step. The spec cannot simultaneously require a fixed 3-way holdout split AND 5-fold CV for the primary validation metric without clarifying which applies to which model or phase.

SC-003 sets a target AUC ≥ 0.75, while US-3 Acceptance Scenario 2 sets a test AUC ≥ 0.70. These success criteria contradict each other regarding the minimum acceptable performance threshold for the held-out test set.

Terminology inconsistency: US-2 Acceptance Scenario 1 lists 'Vocabulary Richness' as a required metric, but FR-002 lists 'Vocabulary Richness' as the 6th feature while US-2 text only explicitly names 'lexical diversity metrics' generally. While likely the same, the spec should explicitly map 'Vocabulary Richness' to a specific calculation (e.g., MTLD or TTR) to ensure the FR and US are describing the exact same entity.
